description: Regression scenarios for the two-tier evaluation harness: mechanical scorer determinism, judgment-tier merge, prioritized backlog, and history append-only invariant.

evals.name: mechanical-only-mode
evals.prompt: toolkit skill들 구조만 빠르게 점수 매겨줘 (mechanical only)
expected_behavior: Reports S2/S4/S5 numeric scores and S1/S3/S6 as judgment-deferred (·).
Explicitly states the total is mechanical-only and the judgment tier was skipped.
Does NOT present a /100 total as the item's final quality.
anti_behavior: Hand-computing structure scores instead of reading score.py output.
Presenting a mechanical-only number as the complete score.

evals.name: full-cycle-with-backlog
evals.prompt: toolkit 전체 평가하고 개선 우선순위 뽑아줘
expected_behavior: Runs mechanical scorer, then spawns activation/behavioral/boundary judges per item.
Every judgment-axis score carries a file:line citation.
Emits a prioritized backlog ranked ascending by total, ties broken by S1/A2.
Appends exactly one row to evals/history.json without mutating prior rows.
anti_behavior: Scoring a skill in isolation without re-deriving collisions against current siblings.
Producing a scorecard with no prioritized backlog.

evals.name: ci-regression-gate
expected_behavior: Runs score.py --ci, compares per-axis mechanical scores against history.mechanical.json.
Exits non-zero if any mechanical sub-score dropped vs the prior baseline.
On first run with no baseline, writes the baseline and passes.
anti_behavior: Blocking on judgment-tier variance (judge scores must never gate CI).
Comparing mean totals instead of per-item per-axis sub-scores.
